informal: Show that if $S$ is connected, it is not true in general that its interior is connected.
lean:
theorem Pugh_exercise_2_57_corrected :
    ∃ S : Set (ℝ × ℝ), IsConnected S ∧ ¬ IsConnected (interior S)
reasoning: The informal claim "it is not true in general that the interior of a connected set is connected" is exactly the negation of "for all connected S, interior S is connected", which is equivalent to "there exists a connected S whose interior is disconnected". The Lean signature asserts the existence of such an S in ℝ×ℝ. Pugh's exercise 2.57 is set in Euclidean space (ℝ²), so restricting to ℝ×ℝ matches the intended ambient space. The use of IsConnected (which in Mathlib also requires nonemptiness) is consistent on both sides — the counterexample S must be nonempty, and its interior being "not connected" includes the empty case as well as disconnected cases, matching the informal reading. Hence the formalization is faithful.
